api_metadata.name: GEM Mine ID
api_metadata.definition: A unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to each coal mine by GEM for internal identification purposes.

api_metadata.name: MSHA ID
api_metadata.definition: A unique numerical identifier assigned to each US coal mine by the US Mine Safety and Health Administration (MSHA) for identification purposes nationwide.

api_metadata.name: Country / Area
api_metadata.definition: The geographic country or area where a coal mine or proposed coal mine project is located.

top_values: Xukuang Group Xinjiang Sai'er Energy Co., Ltd. No.3 Coal Mine

api_metadata.name: Mine Name
api_metadata.definition: The name of a coal mine or proposed coal mine project.
api_metadata.other_rules: Must be unique unless the Mine ID is the same, then we consider it a unit within the mine project parent, duplicates can occur because official Mine names are duplicated within the industry

api_metadata.name: Mine Name AKAs
api_metadata.null_is_what: Not found, Not applicable, Not yet researched
api_metadata.definition: The nickname or alias of a coal mine or proposed coal mine project. AKAs are often used with proposed projects which have not yet been assigned an official name.

api_metadata.name: Mine Name (Non-ENG)
api_metadata.definition: The non-english name of a coal mine or proposed coal mine project, as it appears in the official language of the country or area where the mine is located.

api_metadata.name: Owners
api_metadata.definition: The immediate owner of a coal mine or coal mine project who is responsible for the mining of the coal. The number in brackets next to the owning entity's name denotes the percentage of shares they hold. If an individual rather than a company owns the mine, this is indicated by the term "natural person(s)."

api_metadata.name: Owners (Non-ENG)
api_metadata.definition: The non-english name of the immediate owner of the coal mine or proposed coal mine project, as it appears in the official language of the country or area where the mine is located.

top_values: Government of Telengana [51%]; Government of India [49%]
Ministry of Fuel and Energy of Luhansk People's Republic
Ministry of Energy (Romania) [77.2%]; Fondul Proprietatea SA [21.6%]; other unknown/mixed entity types [1.3%]

api_metadata.name: Parent Company
api_metadata.definition: The highest-level owner of a coal mine or coal mine project. The number in brackets next to the owning entity's name denotes the percentage of shares they hold. If an individual rather than a company owns the mine, this is indicated by the term "natural person(s)."

value_definitions.name: Owner/Parent GEM Entity ID
value_definitions.definition: A static unique identifier assigned by GEM's Global Energy Ownership Tracker team for internal ownership identification purposes.

api_metadata.name: Company HQs
api_metadata.definition: The geographic country or area where the parent company is headquartered in.

api_metadata.name: Capacity (Mtpa)
api_metadata.definition: The maximum amount of coal a mine or mine project is engineered to produce, expressed in millions of tonnes per year.

api_metadata.name: Production (Mtpa)
api_metadata.definition: The amount of coal produced by a mine, expressed in millions of tonnes per year.

api_metadata.name: Year of Production
api_metadata.definition: The year during which the cited coal production occurred. (If coal output for the current year is unavailable, data for the most recently available year is given.)

value_definitions.name: Surface
value_definitions.definition: A coal mine or coal mine project that extracts coal from shallow seams near the earth’s surface.

value_definitions.name: Underground
value_definitions.definition: A coal mine or coal mine project that extracts coal from deep below the earth’s surface via shafts or tunnels. Also known as “UG” mines.

value_definitions.name: Underground & Surface
value_definitions.definition: A coal mine or coal mine project that extracts coal from both underground and surface methods.

api_metadata.name: Mine Type
api_metadata.definition: The classification of coal mines by mining method, including "Surface," "Underground," and combined "Underground & Surface" facilities.
api_metadata.other_notes: should potentially convert from string to array, since there are two types, but mines can be either or both

value_definitions.name: Open Pit
value_definitions.definition: A surface mining method that extracts coal from open pits near ground level. Also known as “open cast” mining.

value_definitions.name: Strip
value_definitions.definition: A surface mining method where the overlying vegetation, rock, and soil (overburden) is removed in order to reach the underlying coal seam, which usually lies a few feet beneath the surface.

value_definitions.name: Mountaintop Removal
value_definitions.definition: A surface mining method that extracts coal at the summit or summit ridge of a mountain, often by using explosives to remove their “tops” and expose underlying coal seams.

value_definitions.name: Board and Pillar
value_definitions.definition: An underground mining method in which seams of coal are partially mined, leaving large pillars of coal intact to support the overlying layers of rock. Also known as “room and pillar.”

value_definitions.name: Longwall
value_definitions.definition: An underground mining method in which a large “wall” of coal is progressively sheared off.

value_definitions.name: Mixed
value_definitions.definition: Indicates a coal mine or coal mine project uses more than one mining method.

api_metadata.name: Mining Method
api_metadata.definition: The specific technique used to extract coal from the earth.

values_definitions.Open Pit: also known as “Open Cast”, refers to mining directly on the ground surface
values_definitions.Strip: is a type of surface mining where the overlying vegetation, rock, and soil (overburden) is removed in order to reach the underlying coal seam, which usually lies a few feet beneath the surface.
values_definitions.Mountain Top Removal: is a mining practice where the tops of mountains are removed, exposing the seams of coal. It is a form of surface mining at the summit or summit ridge of a mountain.
values_definitions.Board and Pillar: also known as “Room and Pillar”, it’s a method of underground mining. In this method, seams of coal are mined partially, leaving large pillars of coal intact to support the overlying layers of rock
values_definitions.Longwall: is a type of underground mining method used to extract coal from a seam that is too deep for open-pit mining

api_metadata.name: Mine Size (Km2)
api_metadata.definition: The total surface area of the mining covered by the coal mining operation, including surface facilities, access roads, overburden dumps, and the underground or open-pit mine footprint.

api_metadata.name: Mine Depth (m)
api_metadata.definition: The vertical distance from the ground surface down to the coal seam that is being mined.

api_metadata.name: Depth Accuracy
api_metadata.definition: Indicates the confidence level in the reported depth of the coal mine. "Exact" means the depth is directly stated in a reliable source, while "Estimated" means it is inferred from averages at the national level in a variety of industry and government reports.

api_metadata.name: Workforce Size
api_metadata.definition: The total number of personnel employed by a coal mining operation, including both direct employees and contractors working on-site.

api_metadata.name: Workforce Accuracy
api_metadata.definition: Indicates the accuracy level of workforce data. "Exact" means the workforce size is explicitly reported in data sources, while "Estimated" refers to figures generated using GEM’s machine learning model when official data is unavailable.

api_metadata.name: Coal Type
api_metadata.definition: The classification of coal based on its physical and chemical properties, particularly its carbon content, calorific value, moisture, volatile matter, and ash content. Global Coal Mine Tracker use British coal type (lignite, subbituminous, bituminous, anthracite).

api_metadata.name: Coal Grade
api_metadata.definition: The classification of coal based on its primary use: -Thermal Coal: Used for power generation or industrial heating. -Metallurgical Coal (Coking Coal): Used for steelmaking and other industrial processes.

api_metadata.name: Opening Year
api_metadata.definition: The year when the mine commenced its commercial operations.

api_metadata.name: Closing Year
api_metadata.definition: The year in which the coal mine is scheduled to close or has already ceased operations.

top_values: Transition to other type of energy
fields.definition_from_readme: The primary factor or circumstance that led to a coal mine ceasing operations. This may include economic factors such as reserve exhaustion, end of permitted mine life, or economic unviability; regulatory or policy-driven closures such as government-mandated shutdowns or environmental regulations; safety-related closures following accidents or hazardous conditions; or operational factors such as consolidation, acquisition, or closure of an associated power plant.
The present condition or land use of a coal mine site following the cessation of mining operations. This reflects what has happened to the site after closure.

api_metadata.name: Coalfield
api_metadata.definition: Coalfield to which a coal mine belongs.

api_metadata.name: Latitude
api_metadata.definition: Location of the project expressed in decimal degrees, assumed to use the WGS84 coordinate system (as in Google Maps).

api_metadata.name: Longitude
api_metadata.definition: Location of the project expressed in decimal degrees, assumed to use the WGS84 coordinate system (as in Google Maps).

api_metadata.name: Location Accuracy
api_metadata.definition: Indicates whether the location is "Approximate" or "Exact". Location is "Approximate" based on using nearest city or inferred from contextual information in the data source. Location is "Exact" when explicitly provided in the source materials.

value_definitions.name: Primary Consumer, DestinationName
value_definitions.definition: The name of the end user or receiving entity of the coal. The project name should match exactly with those documented by GEM to ensure consistency wherever possible.

api_metadata.name: Primary Consumer, Destination
api_metadata.definition: The main end user or receiving entity of the coal produced by the mine. This may include coal power plants, steel plants, export terminals, or industrial facilities that rely on coal as a primary source.

top_values: RG Tanna Coal Terminal at the Port of Gladstone

api_metadata.name: Primary Consumer, Destination Name
api_metadata.definition: The name of the end user or receiving entity of the coal. The project name should match exactly with those documented by GEM to ensure consistency. Renamed from 'Coal Plant, Steel Plant, Terminal' in the May-2026 release; same field, broader scope (any primary consumer/destination, not just plants/terminals).

api_metadata.name: Primary Consumer, Destination GEM Wiki
api_metadata.definition: GEM's Wiki URLs of end user or receiving entity of the coal. Renamed from 'Coal Plant, Steel Plant, Terminal GEM Wiki' in the May-2026 release.

fields.field_name: Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CH4)

api_metadata.name: Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CH4)
api_metadata.definition: The amount of methane emissions from a coal mine as reported by an official source or company reports, expressed in thousands of metric tonnes per year.

fields.field_name: Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CO2 equivalent)

api_metadata.name: Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CO2 equivalent)
api_metadata.definition: The amount of methane emissions from a coal mine as reported by an official source or company reports, expressed in thousands of metric tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalents (CO₂e) per year.
api_metadata.unit_name_long: thousand tonnes CO2 equivalent per year

fields.field_name: Year of Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions

api_metadata.name: Year of Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions
api_metadata.definition: The year in which the coal mine methane emissions were reported or released.
associated_field: Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CH4)
Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CO2 equivalent)

api_metadata.name: Reference Type
api_metadata.definition: Indicates the official source (national government data or company report) where the reported coal mine methane figures were found.

api_metadata.name: Source ID
api_metadata.definition: The identifier assigned by the reporting agency to the coal mine associated with the reported methane emissions.

fields.field_name: Has mine boundary and methane emissions sources data

api_metadata.name: Has associated plume data
api_metadata.definition: A “Yes” in this field indicates that the GCMT asset has been associated with a remotely sensed methane plume detailed in the Global Methane Emitters Tracker. Yes* refers to the one mine (Mandalong Coal Mine) which had methane plumes associated with it in the December 2024 briefing which were not included in the version of GMET public at the time due to minor differences in query methodology.

api_metadata.name: Has associated CMM mitigation data
api_metadata.definition: A “Yes” in this field indicates that the GCMT asset has been associated with a known coal mine methane mitigation project (e.g., pre-mining drainage, flaring, ventilation air methane destruction, utilization, etc.).
